title: Process Termination via the exit() Function
description: A flowchart illustrating the sequence of actions performed when a process calls the exit() function in a UNIX system.
elements: Flush and close all I/O streams (stdio)
Parent process receives SIGCHLD and status
relationships: Sequential flow from one action to the next
exit() performs cleanup before handing off to _exit()
_exit() is the final system call that informs the kernel
Kernel notifies parent process of termination
